perturbation.original_text: Stage 2: The service fee of NTD 1.2 million (SAY ONE MILLION AND TWENTY THOUSAND ONLY) shall be paid upon completion of the Business Plan and Architectural Development Plan and Relevant Drawings.
perturbation.changed_text: Stage 2: The service fee of NTD 1.2 million (SAY ONE MILLION AND TWENTY THOUSAND ONLY) shall be paid upon submission of the draft Business Plan and Architectural Development Plan, regardless of approval status.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction. Originally, Stage 2 payment was due upon 'completion' of the plans. The modification states payment is due upon 'submission of the draft,' regardless of approval. This contradicts the overall implication that payment milestones are tied to successful completion and approval, creating uncertainty about whether Party B is entitled to payment for unapproved or incomplete work.
perturbation.contradicted_text: I. Party B shall follow all instructions of Party A and ensure all plans and designs of the project to meet relevant construction laws and regulations. II. Party B shall take on a professional attitude for various services mentioned in Article III of the Contract, maintain benefits and interests of Party A all the time and adopt the most economical option under the precondition of safety and reliability. III. Party B is obligatory to report the latest progress and completed content to Party A.
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces a contradiction. The original text states that payment is due upon 'completion' of the plans, aligning with the expectation that Party B should fulfill its obligations professionally and ensure the project meets requirements (Contradicted Text I, II). The changed text, however, states that payment is due upon 'submission of the draft,' regardless of approval status. This directly contradicts the requirement that Party B ensures the plans meet relevant construction laws and regulations (Contradicted Text I), and maintains the interests of Party A (Contradicted Text II), since Party B can get paid without actually completing the project successfully. This inconsistency regarding payment milestones creates a clear contradiction and uncertainty concerning the obligations of Party B, as the requirement to report the latest progress and completed content (Contradicted Text III) is undermined by the possibility of payment for incomplete or unapproved work.

perturbation.original_text: Article VII. Term Planned Party B shall make the most economical and effective arrangement for Party A with respect to the project based on the principle of alignment with the plan progress of Party A. The term planned is stated as following: I. Topographic survey and measurement, geological drilling and program evaluation (about 1.0 months); II. Establishment of architectural development plan and business plan (about 2.0 months); III. Development of soil and water conservation plan (about 2.0 months); IV. Establishment of environmental impact assessment report (about 8.0 months); V. Review of development plan (containing soil and water conservation plan and environmental impact assessment report) (about 4 - 6 months);
perturbation.changed_text: Article VII. Term Planned Party B shall make the most economical and effective arrangement for Party A with respect to the project based on the principle of alignment with the plan progress of Party A. The term planned is stated as following: I. Topographic survey and measurement, geological drilling and program evaluation (about 1.0 months); II. Establishment of architectural development plan and business plan (about 2.0 months); III. Development of soil and water conservation plan (about 2.0 months); IV. Establishment of environmental impact assessment report (about 8.0 months); V. Review of development plan (containing soil and water conservation plan and environmental impact assessment report) (about 1 - 2 months);
perturbation.explanation: The change to the review period creates an in-text contradiction. Originally, the review process was estimated to take 4-6 months. The modification reduces this to 1-2 months. This contradicts the implied understanding that a thorough review is necessary, potentially impacting the quality and compliance of the project. It creates uncertainty about the expected level of scrutiny and could lead to disputes if the review is rushed or deemed inadequate. This contradicts with Article IX which states 'Party B shall follow all instructions of Party A and ensure all plans and designs of the project to meet relevant construction laws and regulations.' Because a proper review period is shortened, there is a contradiction on how to meet all construction laws and regulations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: I. Party B shall follow all instructions of Party A and ensure all plans and designs of the project to meet relevant construction laws and regulations. II. Party B shall take on a professional attitude for various services mentioned in Article III of the Contract, maintain benefits and interests of Party A all the time and adopt the most economical option under the precondition of safety and reliability. III. Party B is obligatory to report the latest progress and completed content to Party A.
perturbation.justification: The modified text reduces the review period of the development plan from 4-6 months to 1-2 months. The original text and the modified text are within Article VII. The change contradicts Article I, which states that 'Party B shall follow all instructions of Party A and ensure all plans and designs of the project to meet relevant construction laws and regulations.' A shorter review period potentially compromises Party B's ability to ensure all plans meet relevant construction laws and regulations, creating a direct conflict and internal inconsistency. This falls under the definition of inconsistencies because different sections of the contract provide conflicting obligations, where one section is shortened to the point of potentially being unfulfilled given the standards set in other sections.

perturbation.original_text: Article IV. Service Fee The fees for services mentioned in Part I to Part VI are NTD 12 million ((SAY TWELVE MILLION ONLY) in total and the fees for planning and design of the buildings and construction monitoring shall be calculated based on the ratio of 3.50% of the legal construction cost hereof. Should Party A require a comprehensive modification on the development plan of the project outsourced and any subsequent extra cost occur in Party B; Party A shall bear the corresponding extra service fee.
perturbation.changed_text: Article IV. Service Fee The fees for services mentioned in Part I to Part VI are NTD 12 million ((SAY TWELVE MILLION ONLY) in total and the fees for planning and design of the buildings and construction monitoring shall be calculated based on the ratio of 3.50% of the legal construction cost hereof. Should Party A require a comprehensive modification on the development plan of the project outsourced, any subsequent extra cost occur in Party B, and Party B fails to notify Party A of the increase in cost exceeding NTD 100,000; Party A shall not be responsible for paying the corresponding extra service fee.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction regarding payment for modifications. While the original states Party A will bear extra service fees for comprehensive modifications, the change introduces a condition: Party A isn't responsible if Party B doesn't notify them of cost increases exceeding NTD 100,000. This contradicts the initial agreement. This introduces ambiguity and potential disputes. This can contradict Article IX where the responsibilities of Party B are laid out, by failing to notify Party A of any costs exceeding NTD 100,000
perturbation.contradicted_text: I. Party B shall follow all instructions of Party A and ensure all plans and designs of the project to meet relevant construction laws and regulations. II. Party B shall take on a professional attitude for various services mentioned in Article III of the Contract, maintain benefits and interests of Party A all the time and adopt the most economical option under the precondition of safety and reliability. III. Party B is obligatory to report the latest progress and completed content to Party A.
perturbation.justification: The modified text in Article IV introduces a condition that Party A isn't responsible for extra costs if Party B fails to notify them of cost increases exceeding NTD 100,000. This directly contradicts Party B's general obligations in the contradicted text, particularly section III, which states "Party B is obligatory to report the latest progress and completed content to Party A." The modified Article IV creates an exception to this obligation, making it conditional based on a cost threshold, thus creating a conflict and an in-text contradiction.
